gallery_guess: 玉器展廳-玉之德/玉璧區
ocr_text:
法力無邊的玉璧
玉璧是禮制地位最高的玉器。祭祀之外，還用於招賢納聘及元旦朝賀等儀式中。喪禮中玉璧用以裹尸，綴連在全套玉匣包裹在人頭頂的部位，或釘於棺材頭端，希望引領魂靈通過玉璧中孔，達到「太一」之境。戰國至漢代玉璧上的紋飾有：雲紋、勾連雲紋、穀紋、蒲紋、乳丁紋等。有的還雕琢龍鳳紋，四神紋與吉祥語。
The All-Powerful Jade Bi Disc
The Jade bi disc was the top article in the ritual hierarchy. It was used in sacred worship as well as secular ceremonies, such as in recruiting talent, proposing a marriage, betrothal gifts, new year audience and celebrations at the court, and so on. A funerary suit sewn of jade pieces would wrap the deceased body, with a bi disc specifically for the apex of the head or the head end of the coffin, so the soul would hopefully pass through the central hole of the disc into eternity. The patterns that adorn the jade discs from the Warring States Period to Han dynasty include designs in cloud, cloud scroll, grain, rush mat, embossment, and sometimes with motifs of dragon-phoenix or four-deity, and auspicious phrases.
caption: 玉璧展區大段介紹文「法力無邊的玉璧」
notes: 下方可見一件玉璧邊緣

ocr_text:
嘉慶時的玉器風格與乾隆相去不遠，只是製作數量大減，這種情況到道光時更形明顯。此外，輝玉的使用率增加。
The styles of jades made under Emperor Jiaqing were close to those of the Qianlong reign but in much less quantity. The downturn was ever more apparent when Daoguang was on the throne. Usage of jadeite on the other hand was on the rise.
caption: 清代嘉慶／道光玉器說明文，前景為玉器與「嘉慶年製」款牌
notes: 展品+說明卡，說明卡為展區介紹段落

gallery_guess: 玉器展廳-石英家族區
ocr_text:
五彩繽紛的石英家族
The Colorful Quartz Family
自然界中最容易被發現的美石是石英家族。石英的成分是二氧化矽，又依其晶粒是否能在顯微鏡下被看到，而分為顯晶質與隱晶質（或稱：粗晶與微晶）兩類，可分別稱作水晶與瑪瑙。玉髓因為帶有各種致色色素與內含物等因素，會呈現不同的顏色與外觀。玉髓若帶有條紋、紋狀色斑，就是國人熟悉的瑪瑙。中國境內蘊藏的瑪瑙多又分佈甚廣，距今約五千多年，長江下游的北陰陽營文化、凌家灘文化中，頗多用玉髓、瑪瑙製作的玦飾、璜等裝飾品，甚至還用瑪瑙製作斧頭。在西周時，突然大量出現全紅色的玉髓珠、管，到也玉髓、閃玉等串連成美麗的組珮，本院這類藏品展於「玉之德」單元。
文獻記載，自古就從西域傳入各種瑪瑙，所以，很難從瑪瑙的色澤文理推測其產地。通常色帶平行直線的稱「縞瑪瑙」，紋理細密層層纏繞的稱為「纏絲瑪瑙」，似綠繞瑪瑙就稱作「苔蘚瑪瑙」和「柏枝瑪瑙」。玉髓若含有銅、鎳、鐵等致色色素會呈現藍色、綠色與黃色。前二種本院藏品中沒有見到。院藏名品「三連章」曾被訂名為「黃玉」，距今多年前經比重測定，改名為「黃玉髓」；近日再測得以折射指數與拉曼光譜，更確定其質地為玉髓。完全不透明的玉髓稱為碧石，本院有名的肉形石就是顏色呈帶狀的碧石。
透明無色的水晶中，若含有三價鐵離子就會呈現黃色或紫色。若含有鈦離子，就會呈現粉紅色。若含有鋁離子，且受到微量輻射，破壞了透明水晶的原子結構，就會變成煙水晶（或稱為茶晶），如果太濃就成了墨晶。至於本院有名的髮晶雕馬背猴，就是水晶內包裹了金紅石或電氣石的針狀晶體。內部裂璺等的粉晶、紫晶、煙晶等，在清宮中常被稱作「碧霞」。此間第1至4櫃展出五彩繽紛的石英家族，第5櫃將粉晶、紫晶並列。至於帳冊舊名為「綠水晶」者，近日經測折射指數與拉曼光譜，確定為與祖母綠同一家族的海藍寶石，故與嵌有祖母綠的水晶罐併陳於第9櫃。
caption: 石英家族展區大段中文介紹文「五彩繽紛的石英家族」
notes: 對應 IMG_4753 為同段英文

gallery_guess: 玉器展廳-石英家族區
ocr_text:
The Quartz family boasts beautiful stones that can be most easily found in nature. Mainly consisting in silica, texturally it can be either phaneritic (macrocrystalline) or aphanitic (microcrystalline), and commonly categorized as rock crystal and chalcedony, respectively. Various colorants and inclusions also give quartz different hues and appearances, among which agate is a familiar type of chalcedony featuring colors in bands or masses. China is rich in chalcedony deposits, which are found in many areas across the country. The Beiyinyangying Culture and Linjiatan Culture, both in the lower reach of Yangzi River and dated more than 5000 years ago, had many jue earrings and huang ornaments made of chalcedony. There were even agate fu axes. Then in Western Zhou dynasty, all-red chalcedony beads and tubes suddenly appeared in large quantity; they were threaded with other types of chalcedony as well as nephrite to compose beautiful sets of pei ornaments in pendant style, featuring in the "Virtue of Jade" section of the exhibition.
Literature records show that agate had been imported from Western Regions to China since the remote past. It is difficult to infer the place of origin for a given piece of agate based on its colors and textures. There are several types of agate: onyx, parallel striped; sardonyx, tightly entwined; moss agate, with chlorite inclusions; dentritic agate, with pyrolusite inclusions. Chalcedony with colorant contents such as copper, nickel or iron would appear blue, green, and yellow. The former two are not seen in the Museum collection, whereas the renowned Tri-Seal set was once named as yellow "jade" and have since reclassified as yellow "chalcedony" after a density test, confirmed again recently using the refractive index and Raman spectroscopy. Chalcedony of total opacity is called jasper; the famous meat-shaped stone is of a jasper with banded colors.
Transparent, colorless rock crystals would become tinted by the presence of different ions: ferric iron, yellow or violet; titanium, pink. With aluminum ions, and if exposed to trace radiation altering the crystalline structure, the color would change to grayish brown, called smoky quartz (or brown quartz), or even to darker shades under higher concentration. The well-known carving of monkey riding horse in the Museum collection is a rock crystal of acicular prism included with rutile or tourmaline. Rose quartz, amethyst, and smoky quartz with many inner crack lines were often called "tourmaline" in the Qing court. The cabinets 1 to 4 show the specimens from the colorful quartz family, and cabinet 5 juxtaposes a rose quartz and a tourmaline for comparison. So-called "green crystal" as recorded in the old palace catalogues has been reclassified by the refractive index and Raman spectroscopy as aquamarine, belonging in the same category as emerald. Therefore it is placed in the same cabinet (9) with the emerald-inlaid crystal jar.
caption: 石英家族展區英文介紹文 The Colorful Quartz Family
notes: IMG_4752 中文版的英文對照
